test_description: This test case evaluates the system's ability to retrieve and synthesize information from multiple, separate document sections to answer a complex question.
question: [Fill] In the fan-out wafer-level packaging (FOWLP) process described in Section 1, chips are embedded in _____ and have Cu contact-pads exposed for building up redistribution layers (RDLs) after _____ and backgrinding, whereas in the direct-bonded LED array fabrication process described in Section 11, the LED structure's ultra-flat bonding interface is formed by chemical-mechanical polishing and bonding is facilitated by the presence of _____ on the contacts to enable direct bonding with silicon driver chips.

gold_context_sources.section_title: Fan-OutWafer-Level Packaging(FOWLP)of LargeChipwith Multiple Redistribution Layers (RDLs)
gold_context_sources.evidence: In this study, the chips are embedded in an EMC. The packaging assembly method is by chip-first and die face-up FOWLP processing. The uniqueness of this process is to fabricate Cu contact-pads on the device wafer. These Cu contact-pads are for building the RDLs after post-mold cure (PMC) of the EMC and backgrinding of the EMC.

The example LED structure 200 provides an ultra-flat bonding interface 202, made flat by chemical-mechanical polishing (CMP) for example, with both n contact 108 and p contact 110 surrounded by an insulator 204, such as a silicon oxide, and exposed on the ultra-flat bonding interface 202 with respective coplanar conductive footprints 206 & 208 on the ultra-flat bonding interface 202.

ground_truth_answer: The correct answer is [EMC, post-mold cure (PMC), silicon oxide]. Reasoning: Section 1 explains that in the fan-out wafer-level packaging (FOWLP) process, chips are embedded in an epoxy molding compound (EMC). The Cu contact-pads are exposed after the post-mold cure (PMC) of the EMC and backgrinding of the EMC, which allows building the redistribution layers (RDLs). Section 11 describes a direct-bonded LED array fabrication process where the LED structure has an ultra-flat bonding interface formed by chemical-mechanical polishing (CMP). The n and p contacts are surrounded by an insulator such as silicon oxide, which is exposed on the bonding interface and facilitates direct bonding to silicon driver chips. Hence, the blank spaces are filled with 'EMC' (embedding material), 'post-mold cure (PMC)' (process step exposing Cu pads), and 'silicon oxide' (insulator enabling direct bonding).
